courseleaf.title: Soil Science, Minor
courseleaf.informationtext:
The Department of Land, Air & Water Resources, Soils & Biogeochemistry Program, offers a minor program in soil science. The minor is especially geared toward students in the environmental sciences including Hydrologic Science, Environmental Science & Management,  Environmental Toxicology, Ecological Management & Restoration, International Agricultural Development, Crop Science & Management, Environmental Horticulture & Urban Forestry, Geology, and Plant Biology.

Major Programs

See the Soils & Biogeochemistry track in Environmental Science & Management.

Graduate Study

Programs of study leading to the M.S. and Ph.D. degrees in Soils & Biogeochemistry are available. Information regarding these programs can be obtained from the graduate advisor, at Advising Information and in the Graduate Announcement. See also Graduate Studies.

Lead Faculty Advisor

Anthony (Toby) O’Geen, Ph.D. (Land, Air, & Water Resources)
courseleaf.requirementstext:
Course List
Code Title Units
SSC 100Principles of Soil Science5
Choose 16 units:16
HYD 124
Plant-Water-Soil Relationships
HYD 134
Aqueous Geochemistry
PLS 158
Mineral Nutrition of Plants
SSC 102
Environmental Soil Chemistry
SSC 105
Field Studies of Soils in California Ecosystems
SSC 107
Soil Physics
SSC 109
Sustainable Nutrient Management
SSC 111
Soil Microbiology
SSC 118
Soils in Land Use & the Environment
SSC 120
Soil Genesis, Morphology, & Classification
Total Units21
courseleaf.quickviewtext: The Soil Science minor is geared toward students majoring in the environmental sciences including Hydrologic Science, Environmental Science & Management, Environmental Toxicology, Ecological Management & Restoration, International Agricultural Development, Crop Science & Management, Environmental Horticulture &  Urban Forestry, Geology, and Plant Biology.
